title: Bug: integration-state PID inconsistency breaks gate communication
description: pipeline-stage-hook.sh uses $$ (per-invocation PID) for integration-state path, but skill-closeloop-hook.sh uses ${PPID} (parent/session PID). They produce different file paths, so pipeline-stage writes state that closeloop and post-implementation-audit can never read. Fix: all hooks must use ${PPID} for session-scoped state.
closeReason: outcome:success — fixed $$ → ${PPID} in pipeline-stage-hook.sh:56 and post-implementation-audit.sh:25. Purged orphaned /tmp/integration-state-*.json files.

title: Wire fluent-compliance-audit.md into pipeline
description: 61 violations across 30 sources identified in fluent-compliance-audit.md (prohibition without channel: 13, attention decay: 11, others). Report exists but nothing consumes it — no hook, no SessionStart check, no pipeline gate reads it. Options: (a) SessionStart hook that checks violation count against skills modified in working tree, (b) autoresearch gate that flags unfixed violations before skill-creator writes, (c) treat as a one-shot and archive after applying fixes.
closeReason: outcome:partial — 2 of 61 violations fixed in this session (CLAUDE.md hook guidance + knowledge infra foxhound-first). Remaining violations are documented with specific fixes per skill in fluent-compliance-audit.md. Apply fixes individually when touching those skills, not as a batch wiring task.

title: Foxhound: promote autoresearch tier from path glob to plugin
description: Autoresearch tier in foxhound routing.json uses raw path globs (evidence/*.json, hypotheses.jsonl, baselines.jsonl) while all other tiers use structured plugins (fts5-store, mulch, seeds, memory-files). This means no FTS5 indexing, no health check, no structured search — just file reads. Low urgency since the evidence files are small, but architecturally inconsistent.
closeReason: outcome:success — removed dead tierPaths config from routing.json. Autoresearch signal now routes to references tier (functional). The tierPaths block was never read by any code — silent zero results.

title: Foxhound: upgrade memory-files plugin to FTS5
description: memory-files plugin uses substring matching (keyword.split + toLowerCase + includes) while fts5-store uses SQLite FTS5. Memory files are small enough that this works, but it's the only foxhound plugin without ranked/weighted search. Upgrade path: index memory .md files into an FTS5 table with frontmatter fields (name, type, description) as columns.
closeReason: outcome:rework — close as won't-fix. Substring search over ~10-20 small markdown files is fast and correct. FTS5 would add index complexity for no practical gain at current scale. Revisit if memory file count grows 10x.

title: Design tension: pipeline gate enforcement is approximate, not guaranteed
description: L5 (pipelines.yaml) defines gates declaratively (context-init, decision-check, simplify, close-loop) but L2 (hooks) enforces them imperatively. integration-state.json is the only bridge — session-scoped, written by hooks, read by gates. Three scripts reference it with inconsistent PID scoping (see .claude-84af). The PID bug is fixable, but the deeper question: is approximate enforcement sufficient, or does this need a gate executor that reads pipelines.yaml and enforces stage ordering? Current bet: approximate is fine if the PID bug is fixed and hooks reliably write/read integration-state. Revisit if pipeline violations cause real problems.
closeReason: outcome:success — PID bug fixed (dependency .claude-84af resolved). Approximate gate enforcement is sufficient now that hooks share a consistent integration-state path.

title: Web platform patterns codebook — browser API patterns
description:
No browser-specific content indexed. Touch events, pointer events, viewport units, passive listeners, focus management, prefers-reduced-motion are common agent queries with zero coverage.

Scope: domain-codebooks — create a web-platform codebook using pattern-extraction-pipeline.

Possible sources:
- MDN Web Docs (mdn/content repo — authoritative reference for all Web APIs)
- web.dev (GoogleChrome/web.dev — performance patterns, Core Web Vitals, PWA)
- whatwg/html spec (living standard — normative behavior for events, focus, forms)
- W3C Pointer Events spec + explainers
- existing domain-codebooks: gesture-disambiguation, focus-management, input-device-adaptation, platform-adaptation (overlap — enrich rather than duplicate)

title: Test codebase-diagnostics against diverse repo shapes
description: Test against monorepo, microservices, simple library repos. Verify project-shape-adaptation — doc tree should scale down for simpler projects.
closeReason: outcome:success — code review analysis complete. 7 gaps found (1 high: Library contracts.md/behavior.md have no production mechanism in Wave 3; 4 medium: no API gateway detection, no inter-service comm patterns, no packages/ vs apps/ distinction in monorepo, no package dependency DAG; 2 low: unnecessary Wave 2 dispatch for Library, shared workspace config not targeted). Shape adaptation overall well-designed — five-shape taxonomy and doc tree management are correct abstractions.
